Title: The emergence and loss of cyclic peptides in Nicotiana illuminate dynamics and mechanisms of plant metabolic evolution
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Burpitide (cyclic RiPP peptide) biosynthesis; lyciumin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: BURP-domain burpitide cyclase / lyciumin precursor cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant-specific BURP domain protein; autocatalytic peptide cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected from roots; lyciumins P and Q identified in N. attenuata roots consistent with NatBURP1 chemotype
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Root metabolite profiling by LC-MS; heterologous transient expression in N. benthamiana; site-directed mutagenesis/chimeric cyclase swaps; MS/MS analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Autocatalytically cyclizes encoded core peptide(s) to form lyciumins by installing the characteristic burpitide macrocyclic bond in the lyciumin core peptide
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Encoded lyciumin core peptide motifs such as [QPWGVYSW]
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for lyciumin chemotype; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots in N. attenuata; produced in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves upon expression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant specialized metabolites; precise ecological function not established in this study; burpitides are generally associated with defense and diverse bioactivities
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NatBURP1 is the endogenous N. attenuata lyciumin precursor/cyclase associated with lyciumin P and Q detected in roots. 2. Heterologous expression of NatBURP1 in N. benthamiana reconstituted production of lyciumins P and Q. 3. NatBURP1 displays broader/promiscuous catalytic capacity than the nanamin cyclase, producing nanamin-related cyclic products from nanamin core peptides. 4. Swapping five active-site residues altered substrate specificity between lyciumin and nanamin cyclases.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutagenized versions showed reduced catalytic efficiency; other tradeoffs not reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful peptide macrocyclase scaffold for engineering novel cyclic peptides and agronomic/biotech traits; promiscuity may enable broader substrate engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: De novo transcriptome assembly context in study; no NatBURP1-specific omics accession generated here
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Full lyciumin biosynthesis likely also requires peptidases and glutaminyl cyclotransferases for processing and pyroglutamate formation, but NatBURP1 BURP domain is the cyclase component experimentally validated here

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Burpitide (cyclic RiPP peptide) biosynthesis; nanamin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: BURP-domain burpitide cyclase / nanamin precursor cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant-specific BURP domain protein; autocatalytic peptide cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Pseudogenized locus in N. attenuata; nanamin not detected in N. attenuata tissue extracts
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Repaired construct 5x_Nna transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pseudogene repair/resurrection; heterologous transient expression; LC-MS and MS/MS; purification and NMR structural elucidation; alanine scan and core peptide mutagenesis; chimeric residue swaps
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Autocatalytically cyclizes the heptapeptide core [QPWGLMY] by forming a C–C bond between the tyrosine side-chain phenolic metacarbon and the backbone α-carbon of glycine, yielding nanamin; supports variants with noncritical substitutions
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Encoded nanamin core peptide [QPWGLMY] and multiple engineered variants containing the required glycine and tyrosine positions
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Nanamin A; oxidized nanamin B observed secondarily
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for nanamin chemotype; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Produced in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves; wild-type chemotype accumulates in roots of related Nicotiana species
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precise ecological function not established; notable scaffold for peptide engineering and potential agricultural/drug discovery applications
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables nanamin production and determines nanamin-specific cyclization chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ΨNatBURP2 is a pseudogenized N. attenuata paralog of NatBURP1 lacking native function in planta. 2. Repair of the frameshift and stop codons created 5x_Nna, which restored nanamin-producing cyclase activity in N. benthamiana. 3. The enzyme forms a unique Tyr-to-Gly α-carbon C–C macrocycle in nanamin. 4. The cyclase tolerates many substitutions/insertions/deletions in the core peptide but requires glycine and tyrosine for cyclization. 5. Five active-site residue changes are sufficient to interconvert nanamin- and lyciumin-type cyclase specificities.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves; spontaneous oxidation during purification generated nanamin B
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Native N. attenuata locus is pseudogenized and no nanamin accumulates; engineered/mutant cyclases can show reduced catalytic efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highly valuable macrocyclase scaffold for engineering diverse small cyclic peptides due to broad substrate tolerance and ability to form chemically challenging C–C macrocycles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Construct contains reduced tandem repeat number (5 instead of 11) for synthesis convenience; pyroglutamate formation and peptide processing likely involve host enzymes in addition to the BURP cyclase

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Burpitide (cyclic RiPP peptide) biosynthesis; nanamin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: BURP-domain burpitide cyclase / nanamin precursor cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant-specific BURP domain protein; autocatalytic peptide cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified from assembled N. clevelandii transcriptome; nanamin A/B detected in N. clevelandii roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome assembly; gene identification; transient expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes native nanamin precursor peptide to generate nanamin products analogous to resurrected 5x_Nna activity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for nanamin chemotype; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots of N. clevelandii; produced in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves upon expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NclBURP2 was identified from the N. clevelandii transcriptome as the intact wild-type nanamin precursor gene. 2. N. clevelandii roots contain nanamin A and oxidized nanamin B. 3. Heterologous expression of NclBURP2 in N. benthamiana reproduced nanamin production, validating gene function.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Natural intact nanamin cyclase source for peptide engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome assembly from NCBI sequence reads SRR6918819-SRR6918830 and SRR2913179-SRR2913196; transcript deposited as PV067132
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents unequivocally validated wild-type nanamin precursor retained in a close relative of N. attenuata

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Burpitide (cyclic RiPP peptide) biosynthesis; lyciumin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: BURP-domain burpitide cyclase / lyciumin precursor cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant-specific BURP domain protein; autocatalytic peptide cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified from assembled N. clevelandii transcriptome; lyciumins detected in N. clevelandii roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome assembly plus species metabolite correlation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Inferred lyciumin precursor cyclase associated with lyciumin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with lyciumin chemotype in N. clevelandii
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NclBURP1 was identified in the assembled N. clevelandii transcriptome as the full-length lyciumin precursor gene. 2. N. clevelandii roots contain lyciumins, supporting its role in lyciumin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential additional lyciumin cyclase resource, though not directly functionally tested in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome assembly from NCBI sequence reads SRR6918819-SRR6918830 and SRR2913179-SRR2913196; transcript deposited as PV067131
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core gene because the paper explicitly identifies it as the full-length lyciumin precursor gene in a species retaining the chemotype, though direct heterologous validation in this study focused on NclBURP2
